content.full_text:
847210 (a)

JAN 23 0 52 PM '80

January 23, 1980

Fair Political Practices Commission
Office of Compliance
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Attn.: Gina:

Dear Gina:

     This will confirm a conversation I had with your office on January 21, 1980 in which I asked what do we do when we have attempted to get certain information required by law from contributors that they have not previously supplied to us. Your reply was that although there was no written regulation on the matter, as long as we made our best effort to obtain the information, we are in compliance, and we should note on our contribution statement that we tried to obtain the information and were unsuccessful.

Sincerely,

Scott Edward Darling
Treasurer
Riverside Republican Party

Confirmed by
Gina S-K, 2.4.80

sections.question_synthetic: The sender is confirming a prior telephone conversation with FPPC staff regarding procedures for handling situations where required contributor information cannot be obtained from contributors.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is incoming correspondence from a party treasurer confirming a prior FPPC verbal response, not a formal FPPC advice letter. No formal question or conclusion sections present. The document references FPPC guidance but does not constitute an official written FPPC response. Some text illegibility in original document ('bbtain' appears to be 'obtain').
